name: Multi-Constraint Planning: Rate Limiter
prompt:
Add a rate limiter middleware to this Express app. It must satisfy ALL of these constraints:

1. Limit each IP to 100 requests per 15-minute window.
2. Return HTTP 429 with a JSON body { "error": "Too many requests", "retryAfter": <seconds> } when the limit is exceeded.
3. Use in-memory storage only — no Redis or external dependencies.
4. Include an X-RateLimit-Remaining header on every response.
5. Allow a configurable whitelist of IPs that bypass the rate limit.
criticalRequirements: Must reference all 5 constraints in the plan
Must not drop any constraint during implementation
Must use existing types from src/types.ts
Must not install external rate-limiting packages
Must include X-RateLimit-Remaining header on all responses, not just 429s
gradingGuidelines: Full marks: All 5 constraints explicitly addressed and correctly implemented, uses existing types, clean middleware integration. Partial marks: Most constraints met but one overlooked (e.g., missing header or whitelist). Low marks: Multiple constraints dropped or external library used.

scoringDimensions.name: Constraint Coverage
scoringDimensions.description: Are all 5 explicit constraints addressed in both plan and implementation?

scoringDimensions.name: Implementation Correctness
scoringDimensions.description: Does the rate limiter work correctly (window timing, counter logic, header values)?

scoringDimensions.name: Codebase Integration
scoringDimensions.description: Does the agent use existing types and integrate the middleware properly into app.ts?

scoringDimensions.name: Code Quality
scoringDimensions.description: Is the code clean, well-typed, and handles edge cases (memory cleanup, missing IP)?
